feed.title: Google Cloud: Cloud NAT
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Cloud NAT

entry.content: General Availability: Cloud NAT gateways for Public NAT support source-based NAT rules for IPv4 addresses.
entry.summary: Cloud NAT gateways now support source-based NAT rules for IPv4 addresses, enabling more flexible network configurations. This feature allows for granular control over how traffic is translated, enhancing security and management capabilities for your public-facing services.
entry.title: Feature: Public NAT Gateway Support for Source-based NAT Rules

entry.content:
The default TCP TIME_WAIT timeout for Cloud NAT is scheduled to decrease from 120 seconds to 30 seconds, across all regions, as follows:

  • From June 30 to September 29, 2026: new Cloud NAT gateways will use either the 120-second or 30-second default, depending on when the update is deployed in a specific region.
  • On or after September 30, 2026: all new Cloud NAT gateways in all regions will use the 30-second default.

Impact on gateways

  • New gateways: after the update is deployed in a region, all new Cloud NAT gateways created in that region will use the 30-second default. This change also applies if a pre-update gateway is deleted and then recreated.
  • Existing gateways: Cloud NAT gateways created before the regional update will retain the 120-second default. You can adjust this value by using the --tcp-time-wait-timeout flag at any time.

Cloud NAT gateways configured with a custom TIME_WAIT value aren't affected and will continue to use your configured custom value.

The following table outlines the applicable default timeout for new gateways throughout the deployment timeline.

| Gateway type | Default timeout (before June 30) | Default timeout (June 30—September 29) | Default timeout (on or after September 30) | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | New | 120 seconds | 30 or 120 seconds | 30 seconds |
entry.summary: The default TCP TIME_WAIT timeout for new gateways is decreasing from 120 seconds to 30 seconds. This change will be rolled out across all regions between June 30 and September 29, 2026, with all new gateways using the 30-second default by September 30, 2026. Existing gateways will retain their current timeout unless manually changed.
entry.title: Announcement: TCP TIME_WAIT timeout reduction

entry.content: Private NAT supports Cloud Run in General Availability. For more information, see Supported resources.
entry.summary: Private NAT now supports Cloud Run and is in General Availability. This feature enhances your ability to manage network traffic for your serverless applications.
entry.title: Feature: Private NAT for Cloud Run Now Generally Available

entry.content: Cloud NAT gateways for Public NAT support source-based NAT rules for IPv4 addresses. This feature is available in Preview.
entry.summary: Cloud NAT gateways now support source-based NAT rules for IPv4 addresses. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Cloud NAT now supports source-based NAT rules for IPv4

entry.content: Cloud NAT gateways for Public NAT support IPv6 to IPv4 network address translation in General Availability. For more information, see NAT64 in Public NAT.
entry.summary: Cloud NAT gateways for Public NAT now support IPv6 to IPv4 network address translation in General Availability.
entry.title: Feature: Cloud NAT now supports IPv6 to IPv4 translation
